channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Biggest VAR call ever? Four chaotic minutes that may define the season
item.description: The most consequential decision in the history of VAR in the Premier League is played out over four minutes 11 seconds of drama at London Stadium.

item.title: Arsenal the champions in waiting but doing it the hard way
item.description: This season hasn't always been easy on the eye, but Arsenal are on the brink of their first Premier League title in 22 years.

item.title: The reasons West Ham face relegation - and why they are unlucky
item.description: When the dust settles on the West Ham goal that was ruled out against Arsenal, the cold reality is they are now probably facing relegation.

item.title: Why Man City would be foolish to let Shaw leave
item.description: Khadija Shaw needed just 12 minutes to prove why Manchester City must not let her leave in the summer.

item.title: Celtic eye own fairytale as Hearts' story continues to captivate
item.description: Celtic have taken care of Rangers and are coming for Hearts. Can Derek McInnes' side hold them off in the final days of a tumultuous campaign?

item.title: Arsenal edge closer to title after late West Ham drama
item.description: Leandro Trossard's 83rd-minute goal is enough to give Premier League leaders Arsenal a dramatic 1-0 win, as West Ham United are denied a stoppage-time equaliser by VAR at the London Stadium.

item.title: Burnley end losing streak with draw against Villa
item.description: Burnley end a run of five-straight Premier League defeats as they draw 2-2 with Aston Villa at Turf Moor.

item.title: Anderson scores late equaliser as Forest move closer to safety
item.description: Elliot Anderson scores against his former club to earn Nottingham Forest a 1-1 draw against Newcastle United and a seven-point cushion from the Premier League relegation zone.

item.title: Everton's European push dented with draw at Palace
item.description: Everton's push to finish in a European spot suffer a setback as Crystal Palace twice come from behind to pick up a 2-2 draw at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Manchester City beat stubborn Brentford to keep title race alive
item.description: Goals from Jeremy Doku, Erling Haaland and Omar Marmoush help Manchester City beat Brentford 3-0 at the Etihad Stadium to secure a vital win in the Premier League title race, as City move two points behind leaders Arsenal with three games remaining.

item.title: I will not let racist abuse define me - Danso
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur defender Kevin Danso says he will not be defined by racist abuse after being targeted on social media.

item.title: Anderson shows character to go with talent after personal loss
item.description: Elliot Anderson's crucial equaliser against Newcastle for Nottingham Forest was even more admirable given the huge personal loss he has suffered.

item.title: Howe 'looking at the future' with Gordon omission
item.description: Newcastle United head coach Eddie Howe says he left forward Anthony Gordon on the bench for the second week running because he is "looking at the future".

item.title: Are Celtic now favourites to win Scottish Premiership?
item.description: Hearts have led the Scottish Premiership since October and remain one point ahead with two games to go - but are Celtic now favourites for the title?

item.title: Why Brighton's first Women's FA Cup final felt like it was coming
item.description: Brighton reach their first Women's FA Cup final - and within one victory of a first piece of major silverware in the club's history.

item.title: Arteta praises officials' 'courage' for overturning West Ham equaliser
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta praised the officials for overturning West Ham's stoppage-time equaliser as his side picked up a crucial 1-0 victory at the London Stadium.

item.title: 'Where's the consistency?' - Bowen frustrated by VAR
item.description: West Ham captain Jarrod Bowen criticises the consistency of VAR after his side had a late equaliser ruled out for a foul on David Raya during their 1-0 defeat by Arsenal at the London Stadium.

item.title: Lack of consistency in officiating upsetting - Espirito Santo
item.description: West Ham manager Nuno Espirito Santo says the lack of consistency from the officials is upsetting as his side had a late equaliser against Arsenal overturned for a foul on David Raya.

item.title: Pereira hails Forest 'spirit' as unbeaten run secures safety
item.description: Nottingham Forest manager Vitor Pereira hails the "spirit" of his players as they extend their unbeaten run in the Premier League with a 1-1 draw against Newcastle.

item.title: Fans 'losing patience' with Slot as Liverpool 'lack identity' - Rooney
item.description: Speaking on Match of the Day, Wayne Rooney says Liverpool have "no identity" and "the players look disinterested", adding the club's supporters are "losing patience" with manager Arne Slot following their 1-1 draw at home to Chelsea in the Premier League.

item.title: World Cup debutants to wear collectable patches on shirts
item.description: All players who make their first tournament appearance at this summer's Fifa World Cup will wear a debut patch on their shirts.

item.title: 'The goals are coming' - is Doku keeping Man City in title race?
item.description: Jeremy Doku said last month that he needed to add more goals to his game to be considered one of the best wingers in the world, so is he part of that conversation now?

item.title: Hearts & Celtic seal Champions League qualifier spots
item.description: Hearts will play in the Champions League qualifiers next season for the first time in 20 years as Celtic's win over Rangers guarantees a top-two Scottish Premiership finish.

item.title: Boos, no intensity and no identity - who are Slot's Liverpool?
item.description: More boos at Anfield highlight why Liverpool must change their style during a seismic summer for Arne Slot.

item.title: What next for Kieran Trippier?
item.description: Newcastle United defender Kieran Trippier has an open mind about his next move as he focuses on finishing his St James' Park stay on a high.

item.title: Everton not out of European chase yet - Moyes
item.description: David Moyes believes Everton are still in the hunt for European football but need to "step up" their pursuit, having gone five Premier League games without a win following a 2-2 draw with Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park.

item.title: 'Fantastic' Villa deserved to win - Emery
item.description: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery says his side was "fantastic" and deserved all three points following a 2-2 with Burnley at Turf Moor.

item.title: 'Scoring goals isn't about being a genius' - Guardiola
item.description: Manchester City boss Pep Guardiola praised the impact of substitutes Phil Foden and Omar Marmoush, saying "scoring goals isn't about being a genius, it's bringing balls close to Erling Haaland" after his side beat Brentford 3-0, with all the goals coming in the second half.

item.title: 'The Doku show' - Why Man City's winger is crucial for title hopes
item.description: Wayne Rooney says Jeremy Doku has been 'incredible' for Manchester City and his recent goal-scoring form has helped transform the winger into a "world-class" player who can help Manchester City win the Premier League.

item.title: Arsenal secure Champions League spot with Russo-inspired win over Villa
item.description: Alessia Russo stars as Arsenal beat Aston Villa 3-0 in the WSL at Villa Park to confirm their place in next season's Women's Champions League.

item.title: Race for Europe: How it stands and remaining games
item.description: As the Premier League season nears its conclusion, who are favourites to secure a place in Europe next season, and how many English teams could qualify?

item.title: Lacking depth, little threat - Man Utd will not find replacing Casemiro easy
item.description: Manchester United have failed to win any of the four Premier League games Casemiro has failed to start this season, leaving Michael Carrick to ponder how to replace him when he leaves this summer.

item.title: How could extreme weather affect World Cup 2026?
item.description: With almost a month to go until the start of the World Cup, Simon King looks ahead to the weather's potential to disrupt the tournament this summer.

item.title: 'Every team should be angry' - Boro boss condemns Saints over 'spygate'
item.description: Middlesbrough head coach Kim Hellberg describes Southampton's alleged spying on a training session as an attempt to cheat.

item.title: How Bournemouth are on brink of Europe despite losing stars
item.description: Bournemouth have brushed off being raided for their star players and are now on the brink of a European spot for next season.

item.title: Spirited Chelsea stop rot with FA Cup final blueprint
item.description: Chelsea were facing the prospect of becoming the first team in English football history to lose seven consecutive league matches heading into an FA Cup final when they fell 1-0 behind at Anfield, but they found a way out.

item.title: Bonuses, players on the beach & the phoney war - Pulis' end-of-season insights
item.description: Tony Pulis on why motivating players who have nothing to play for is just one of the challenges facing a manager at the end of the season.

item.title: How Le Mans rose from sixth tier to cusp of top-flight return
item.description: Le Mans is most commonly known for its 24-hour race, but the city's football club is backed by big-name sportsmen and on a rapid rise.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v boxing world champion Fabio Wardley
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on world champion boxer Fabio Wardley plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's fixtures.

item.title: Fernandes: The trophies, the assist record and his future
item.description: No Premier League medal, no Champions League title, but Bruno Fernandes has won other trophies and many accolades, and the FWA footballer of the year is on the brink of setting a record.

item.title: How Villa could get sixth into the Champions League
item.description: A host of Premier League clubs will be backing Aston Villa to win the Europa League - as it could secure Champions League qualification for whoever finishes sixth.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: 'Bologna target Verona's Bowie' - gossip
item.description: Bologna reportedly target Hellas Verona striker Kieron Bowie as Liam Scales says he had no interest in leaving Celtic despite interest from other clubs.

item.title: Unmasking the coach who led Man City to WSL title glory
item.description: Who is the Swedish mastermind who has guided Manchester City to a first Women's Super League title for a decade?

item.title: Chelsea favourites to sign Shaw as Man City exit nears
item.description: Chelsea are favourites to sign striker Khadija Shaw on a free contract as an exit from Manchester City nears this summer.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
